methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Comand AI is a seed-stage defense and security software company that has raised €11.5M to date. The comparable universe comprises 10 private and public peers in the Defense & Security sector, including public benchmarks (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and leading private competitors such as Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The Defense & Security sector contains 19 same-sector peers overall, but insufficient clustering exists within narrowly defined cohorts—zero seed-stage same-sector comparables, one adjacent-stage peer, and zero similar-valuation-tier peers were identified. This fragmentation means a peer-median approach would lack the minimum three datapoints required for statistical reliability and would risk material distortion if forced to pull from the broader all-sector population.

The analysis therefore cannot support a triangulated valuation range at this stage. The key uncertainties constraining valuation work are the sparsity of seed-stage precedent transactions in this sector and the wide dispersion in funding, business model, and defensibility across available private comparables. Meaningful valuation would require either accumulation of additional seed-round transaction data within Defense & Security, or primary diligence on growth trajectory, customer traction, and competitive positioning relative to the named peer set.
